title: Uunu

{{Quote|Tell me. Would a ''Jeedai'' be concerned about the fate of a Shamed One? As concerned as he would be for a person of a high caste?|Uunu to Anakin Solo|<ref name="Victory" />}}
'''Uunu''' was a [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Shamed Ones|Shamed One]] who was alive in [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], when the Yuuzhan Vong were [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invading]] a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] other than their [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|own]]. Still young at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the war, Uunu served as a field hand working with [[lambent]]s on the [[Invasion of Yavin 4|captured]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], and it was there that she was assigned to be the overseer of the the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Solo]], while he was disguised as a [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] individual under the alias Bail Lars. During their time together, which ended when Solo left to rescue his friend [[Tahiri Veila]] from the Yuuzhan Vong forces on Yavin 4, Uunu and Solo talked of many things, varying from the nature of the Yuuzhan Vong to the ideals of the [[New Jedi Order]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|It's difficult to imagine all of those worlds, each as big or bigger than this one. I grew up on one of the poorest worldships. There was little room. We lived very close together. Here, there is nothing but space.|Uunu|<ref name="Victory" />}}
[[File:AnakinSolo_EA.jpg|thumb|right|175px|Anakin Solo, who Uunu knew as Bail Lars]]
A [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], Uunu grew up on [[Unidentified Koros-Strohna|one]] of the poorer and more cramped [[Koros-Strohna]], a type of worldship that the Yuuzhan Vong employed, as the [[Species/Legends|species]] traveled from [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|their galaxy]] to [[The galaxy/Legends|another one]] in order to wage [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]] upon its inhabitants. At some point in her life, Uunu became one of the [[Shamed Ones]], meaning that her body would not accept alterations that would further her status, and because of this, she was given hard labor as a job. In [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], a young Uunu was one of the many Shamed Ones and Yuuzhan Vong's [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] individuals who were taken to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] after it was [[Invasion of Yavin 4|captured]] from the [[New Jedi Order]], the [[Jedi/Legends|members]] of whom her people knew as the ''Jeedai''; and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="Victory" />

While on Yavin 4, Uunu was assigned to work in the fields growing [[lambent]]s, which were [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] that the Yuuzhan Vong used for various tasks. It was also there that Uunu realized that the [[Warrior caste|Yuuzhan Vong warrior]] [[Vasi]] had an attraction to her. However, although he could technically command her because of her lower rank, Uunu knew that Vasi wanted her to come to him, because she would make Vasi [[Death/Legends|kill]] her if the [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] commanded Uunu to do anything. One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], while Uunu was working the fields, Vasi assigned her an enslaved [[Human/Legends|Human]] by the name of [[Anakin Solo|Bail Lars]], whom the Shamed One [[Vua Rapuung]] had brought to the fields, after Vasi saw them faint in pain. Although he was supposed to help her fill her quota of lambents, Vasi gave Uunu the slave to hinder rather than help her.<ref name="Victory" />

Uunu taught Lars, who was actually the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] Anakin Solo in disguise, how to tend to the lambents. While she attuned the blossoms of the lambents by stroking them, Solo harvested the ones that already had been attuned. By the end of the first day, Solo had fallen quite behind Uunu. At some point in [[Time/Legends|time]], Uunu voiced her fears that Solo was a Jedi to the warriors, as he did not act as a enslaved individual should, and because of this, they watched the pair closely. The next day, while it was still dark, Uunu picked up Solo from the [[Yavin 4 dormitory|houses]] and showed him how the lambents could be used to produce luminescence, and explained how they worked. During the six [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] of work that followed thereafter, in which they did not stop for rest, Uunu warmed up to the enslaved individual during their conversations about the Yuuzhan Vong and the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], although her people considered Solo an infidel. At lunch, she broached the question about Solo being a Jedi, and after he lied that he was not, the pair discussed the New Jedi Order. It was during this chat that Uunu became more inclined towards the ideals of the Jedi.<ref name="Victory" />

Solo left Uunu silently that night, and after fixing his [[Anakin Solo's lightsaber|lightsaber]] with a lambent crystal using the knowledge Uunu had taught him, went on to rescue his Human Jedi friend [[Tahiri Veila]], who had been captured by the Yuuzhan Vong–affiliated [[Peace Brigade]] group on Yavin 4 and was being held by the Yuuzhan Vong [[Shaper caste|shapers]]. Shortly thereafter, Solo, along with forces under the command of the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Talon Karrde]], as well as several Jedi Knights, destroyed the Yuuzhan Vong military forces and landed to free the enslaved individuals on Yavin 4. However, after the Jedi–smuggler strike, [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] had many of the Shamed Ones on the moon killed, because the [[Jeedai heresy|''Jeedai'' heresy]] was created there. The ''Jeedai'' heresy was a belief among Shamed Ones that the Jedi would rescue them because the Jedi Solo had redeemed Rapuung, who then died defending that same Jedi.<ref name="Victory" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Shamed Ones must show usefulness and humility. We do the work no true caste Yuuzhan Vong may dirty their hands with. If we do not do these things, we are not worth feeding.|Uunu|<ref name="Victory" />}}
As a Shamed One, Uunu's body did not accept the alterations that would allow her to rise in rank and signify her dedication to the [[Yun'o|gods]] of her people. At the time of her meeting with Anakin Solo, Uunu had no obvious scars and appeared deeply worn, though her [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were brighter than those of most of the other Shamed Ones. Because she lived on a cramped worldship when she was young, Uunu wondered at the size of the galaxy that they inhabited, and how there was so much space. As with the majority of Yuuzhan Vong society, after they invaded the other galaxy, she viewed the machinery that the galaxy's inhabitants used as abominations. Because she believed in the will of the gods, she was also of the belief that the Yuuzhan Vong were taking the right path in attacking the galaxy.<ref name="Victory" />

Yet although most Yuuzhan Vong had only contempt for the people whom they were engaging in war, which they called infidels, Uunu warmed up to Solo during their time together. Though she did warn him to watch his tongue when they discussed important matters concerning her people and his, she was much more open to him than most Yuuzhan Vong. Additionally, she did not despise the Jedi and questioned Solo on the nature of the Jedi, and whether they would be concerned with the plight of the Shamed Ones. Uunu believed that she was from [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] born a Shamed One, but continued to hope that some deed of hers, such as potentially turning Solo over to the warriors if in fact he was a Jedi, would increase her favor before the gods and benefit her rank. Uunu considered Vasi sick, because unlike other Yuuzhan Vong warriors, he was attracted to Shamed Ones, over whom he was in a position of power. However, Vasi did not command Uunu because she would make him kill her if he did so. Additionally, although Vua Rapuung thought he was not a Shamed One, she believed that he was indeed one.<ref name="Victory" />

==Behind the scenes==
Uunu first appeared in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'', a novel by [[Greg Keyes]] that was released in [[2001]] as part of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series.<ref name="Victory" />

==Appearances==
*''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'' {{1st}}
